page: Microsoft Azure EC8as_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the ECASv5 server family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server type does not include local storage, meaning it relies entirely on external block storage, though it is designated as Premium Storage capable. It does not feature any integrated GPUs, nor does it include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio, the EC8as_v5 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-performance local disk storage or GPU acceleration. It is suited for workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching, data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute resources.
description: Microsoft Azure EC8as_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the ECASv5 family, utilizing an AMD processor and the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory, establishing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to external storage volumes. It contains no GPUs and offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. With its high memory allocation relative to compute cores, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It is suited for database hosting, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM without the need for local NVMe drives or hardware accelerators.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC8as_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated AMD vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. It is Premium Storage capable and optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure EC8as_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 8 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, running on Hyper-V.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual machine featuring eight dedicated vCPUs and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for memory-intensive workloads.
